title: Aureline embedded-auth exception register record
description: Boundary schema for the embedded-auth exception register frozen in docs/auth/auth_handoff_interstitial_contract.md. The register is the auth-lane source of truth for every approved embedded auth path: a system-browser-first deployment lands here only when an explicit register entry pins the exact reason, the allowed provider/domain, the fallback rule, and the visibly lower-trust presentation requirements. The record composes with the embedded-surface boundary record family (schemas/ux/embedded_surface_boundary.schema.json) — every register entry MUST be quotable by the host-rendered embedded_auth_exception_record carried on a boundary card — and with the auth-handoff interstitial (schemas/auth/auth_handoff_interstitial.schema.json), which cites the register entry id when its auth_flow_class is embedded_session_refresh or embedded_password_exception. Raw provider URLs, raw cookies, raw passwords, raw passkey material, raw nonces, raw PKCE verifiers, and raw provider error bodies never cross this boundary; every reference is opaque-id-aliased and every label is export-safe. Live IdP/OAuth/SAML/passkey integrations are out of scope. Adding a new exception reason, scope class, fallback rule class, lower-trust cue, review state, or audit-event id is additive-minor and bumps embedded_auth_exception_register_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row co-signed by security_trust_review and the owning lane.

embedded_auth_exception_register_schema_.description: Integer schema version for this embedded-auth exception register export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, and safe in support/export packets.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

export_safe_label.description: Short, reviewable label safe to render on banners, support exports, release evidence, and audit streams.

auth_flow_class.description: Auth-flow class re-exported from ADR-0015 and the system-browser callback packet.

approved_embedded_flow_class.description: Closed embedded-flow class authorised by this register entry. system_browser, device_code, and platform_authenticator_native are NEVER admissible here; they do not require a register entry because they are the supported defaults.

auth_exception_reason.description: Closed exception-reason vocabulary re-exported from ADR-0015. Each reason names one and only one cause for departing from the system-browser-first default.

exception_scope_class.description: Closed scope-class vocabulary re-exported from ADR-0015. session_refresh_only authorises a narrow renewal inside an already-authenticated session; limited_account_link authorises one account-link without widening scope; no_high_risk_actions guarantees the embedded path can never raise trust, verify updates, confirm rollback, or apply AI changes.

surface_family.description: Embedded-surface family vocabulary re-exported from ADR-0015.

requested_action_class.description: Requested-action vocabulary re-exported from the auth-handoff interstitial.

lower_trust_cue.description: Closed lower-trust cue vocabulary re-exported from ADR-0015 and extended for the auth-lane register. Cues MUST render visibly; tooltip-only or inspector-only disclosure is non-conforming.

capability_limitation.description: Closed capability-limitation vocabulary the register entry guarantees the host shell will display next to any flow that quotes it.

fallback_rule_class.description: Closed fallback-rule vocabulary. The fallback rule names what the host MUST offer when the embedded path is unavailable, declined, or revoked.

preferred_flow_order.description: Ordered preferred-flow list. The first entry is the supported default the host should offer when the embedded path is not strictly required (typically system_browser, then device_code, then platform_authenticator_native, then the approved embedded flow last).

no_fallback_admissible.description: True only when the provider strictly forbids any non-embedded fallback (rare, requires temporary_enterprise_override or legacy_provider_requires_embedded_password reason). When true, the entry MUST still preserve continue_local_without_sign_in as a typed reject-action outcome on the upstream interstitial.

fallback_summary_label.description: Reviewable single-sentence summary of the fallback rule for support, admin, and release-evidence exports.

fallback_rule.description: Fallback rule the register entry guarantees. Surfaces never invent their own fallback ordering.

lower_trust_badge_required.description: Whether a visible lower-trust badge is required next to the embedded auth surface. Always true for embedded_password_exception; required where the deployment is enterprise_online or managed_cloud.

host_native_step_up_required.description: Whether the host shell MUST require a host-native step-up sheet before any high-risk action that follows from the embedded auth path.

presentation_summary_label.description: Reviewable label support, admin, and release-evidence surfaces quote so a register entry never re-invents its own presentation copy.

no_password_persistence_required.description: Whether the embedded path MUST NOT persist any entered credential to the credential store. Required true for embedded_password_exception.

lower_trust_presentation.description: Visibly lower-trust presentation requirements. The register entry is the auth-lane source of truth; embedded boundary cards re-render these cues by ref rather than minting their own.

exception_review_state.description: Closed register-entry review-state vocabulary. Only active and (briefly) expiring entries authorise an in-product embedded auth path; draft, expired, and revoked entries MUST fail closed with a typed denial reason.

audit_event_id.description: Closed audit-event id vocabulary for the register lane. Audit rows carry typed vocabulary only; raw passwords, raw cookies, raw provider URLs, and raw passkey material are forbidden.

boundary_record_exception_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the host-rendered embedded_auth_exception_record carried on the embedded-surface boundary card schema. Null only for register entries that have not yet been rendered on a boundary card. The two records share vocabulary; this register entry is the auth-lane source of truth.

allowed_provider_domain_label.description: Provider DNS domain label authorised by this register entry (for example, 'login.legacy-acme.example'). Wildcards MUST NOT appear; one register entry authorises one provider domain. Adding a sibling domain is a new register entry, not a wildcard expansion.

connected_provider_record_id.description: Opaque ref to the upstream connected-provider record this register entry is bound to. Null only for entries created before any provider record was minted (rare, requires draft review state).

applies_to_surface_families.description: Closed list of surface families this register entry authorises an embedded auth path on. embedded_auth_confirmation is the typical entry; extension_hosted_surface is admissible only when the extension publisher boundary explicitly accepts the lower-trust cues.

applies_to_action_classes.description: Closed list of requested-action classes this register entry authorises. step_up_authority, admin_step_up, scope_grant, switch_org_or_tenant, and deprovision_acknowledge are forbidden inside this list because they cross a product-owned/native boundary.

host_native_approval_required.description: Whether the host shell MUST require a host-native review/approval surface before any downstream high-risk action. Always true for embedded_password_exception. Required true when applies_to_action_classes includes any class that admits credential entry.

review_owner.description: Reviewer or review-team identifier (for example, 'security_trust_review'). Raw email addresses, raw user ids, and raw directory attributes MUST NOT appear.

review_expiry_at.description: Monotonic timestamp the register entry expires. The host shell MUST emit an expiring warning before this time and MUST fail closed afterward.

revoked_reason_label.description: Reviewable revocation reason. Null while review_state is draft, active, or expiring.

audit_event_refs.description: Refs to audit-event rows emitted by this register entry.

diagnostics_quote_refs.description: Refs the diagnostics, support, and docs/help surfaces quote so the register entry copy is reusable without special-case wording per surface.

notes.description: Optional reviewable note for the register entry. Raw provider error bodies, raw URLs, raw passwords, raw cookies, and raw passkey material MUST NOT appear.

allOf.description: embedded_password_exception MUST require host-native approval, lower-trust badge, and no-password-persistence cues.
embedded_session_refresh MUST forbid password-collection cues and MUST limit scope to session_refresh_only.
applies_to_action_classes MUST NOT include any product-owned/native action that crosses a host-native boundary.
exception_scope_class no_high_risk_actions MUST NOT include link_account or credential_recovery in applies_to_action_classes (those flows widen authority differently and require their own scope).
Revoked entries MUST carry a revocation timestamp and reason label.

embedded_auth_exception_register_record.description: Embedded-auth exception register record. One record per approved embedded auth path; the register is the auth-lane source of truth that the embedded-surface boundary card schema mirrors. Surfaces NEVER mint their own exception copy; they quote the register entry by id.
